Project Structure

Repository Layout

  • Directoryneo-llvm/
    • Directorywasm-neovm/ Core Wasm-to-NeoVM translator
      • …
    • Directoryrust-devpack/ Rust SDK for contract development
      • Directoryneo-macros/ Procedural macros (#[neo_contract], #[neo_method], etc.)
        • …
      • Directoryneo-runtime/ Runtime bindings and storage API
        • …
      • Directoryneo-syscalls/ NeoVM syscall wrappers
        • …
      • Directoryneo-types/ Contract types (NeoInteger, NeoByteString, etc.)
        • …
      • Directoryneo-test/ Contract testing framework
        • …
    • Directorysolana-compat/ Solana compatibility layer
      • …
    • Directorymove-neovm/ Move bytecode translator (experimental)
      • …
    • Directorycontracts/ 17 example contracts
      • …
    • Directoryintegration-tests/ Neo Express integration tests
      • …
    • Directoryscripts/ Build helpers
      • build_contract.sh Build a single Rust contract
      • build_c_contract.sh Build a C contract (experimental)
      • neoexpress_deploy.sh Deploy to local Neo Express
    • Directorydocs/ Technical documentation
      • …
    • Directoryspec/ Formal LaTeX specification
      • …
    • Directorywebsite/ This documentation site (Astro + Starlight)
      • …
    • Makefile Build automation
    • CHANGELOG.md Release history
    • CONTRIBUTING.md Contribution guidelines
    • SECURITY.md Security policy

Component Overview

wasm-neovm

The core translator. It parses a .wasm binary, walks the Wasm instruction stream, and emits equivalent NeoVM opcodes. The output is a NEF binary and a manifest JSON file.

Key responsibilities:

  • Wasm function and type section parsing.
  • Opcode-level translation (Wasm → NeoVM).
  • Memory model mapping (linear memory → NeoVM slots).
  • Table and indirect call handling.
  • NEF serialization with checksum and header generation.
  • Manifest generation from exported functions and embedded overlays.

rust-devpack

The Rust SDK that contract authors depend on. It is split into focused sub-crates:

CratePurpose
neo-macrosProcedural macros that generate boilerplate: #[neo_contract], #[neo_method], #[neo_event], #[neo_safe], #[neo_entry], #[neo_storage], and neo_manifest_overlay!.
neo-runtimeHigh-level runtime API — NeoRuntime (triggers, calling script, time), NeoStorage (get/put/delete/find), and contract call helpers.
neo-syscallsLow-level wrappers around NeoVM system calls. Maps Rust function signatures to the syscall interface the VM expects.
neo-typesContract-oriented types: NeoInteger, NeoByteString, NeoString, NeoMap, NeoArray, NeoIterator, NeoValue, and error types.
neo-testTesting framework for running contract logic in a simulated environment without deploying to a chain.

solana-compat

An experimental compatibility layer that maps Solana program concepts (accounts, program IDs, instruction data) to Neo N3 equivalents. Allows developers familiar with Solana to write contracts using a Solana-like API that compiles to NeoVM.

move-neovm

An experimental translator that reads Move bytecode and emits NeoVM instructions. This is a separate pipeline from the Rust/Wasm path and is at an early stage.

contracts/

Seventeen example contracts demonstrating progressively complex patterns:

  • hello-world — minimal contract, single method.
  • nep17-token — fungible token (NEP-17 standard).
  • nep11-nft — non-fungible token (NEP-11 standard).
  • multisig-wallet — multi-signature authorization.
  • escrow — conditional fund release.
  • staking-rewards — time-based reward distribution.
  • governance-dao — proposal and voting system.
  • crowdfunding — goal-based fundraising with refunds.
  • timelock-vault — time-locked asset storage.
  • oracle-consumer — off-chain data via Neo Oracle.
  • nft-marketplace — listing, bidding, and settlement.
  • flashloan-pool — uncollateralized single-transaction loans.
  • And more.

Each contract has its own Cargo.toml and can be built independently.

Workspace Structure

neo-llvm does not use a Cargo workspace. Each crate has an independent Cargo.toml and is built with an explicit --manifest-path flag. This keeps crate boundaries strict and avoids unintended dependency coupling.

The Makefile at the repository root provides convenience targets that orchestrate builds across crates.

Next Steps

  • Compilation Pipeline — detailed walkthrough of each translation stage.
  • Translator Internals — how wasm-neovm works under the hood.
  • SDK Overview — full reference for the rust-devpack API.
